fields.label: Age
fields.help: Select the age category of the person choking.

explanation:
**Choking emergency response** is based on age-specific guidelines from international health organizations. Proper technique varies by age to ensure safety and effectiveness.

## How it's calculated

Age-based guidelines determine the appropriate Heimlich maneuver or alternative technique for choking emergencies.

This calculator applies evidence-based recommendations based on your selection, ensuring you get age-appropriate guidance.

## Technique by age group

| Age group | Primary technique |
|---|---|
| Adult | Abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver) |
| Child (1-8 years) | Abdominal thrusts, adjusted for smaller body |
| Infant (under 1 year) | Alternating back blows and chest thrusts |
| Pregnant or obese | Chest thrusts (not abdominal) |

## Final notes

This calculator provides quick reference guidance. For actual choking emergencies, formal first aid training is essential. When in doubt, call emergency services (911 in the US, 107 in Argentina). Information is current to 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: The Heimlich maneuver is a first aid technique using abdominal thrusts to dislodge food or objects blocking the airway in a choking adult or child.

faq.q: How do you perform the Heimlich maneuver on an adult?
faq.a: Stand behind the person, wrap your arms around their waist, place a fist above the navel and below the ribcage, and perform quick upward thrusts.

faq.q: Is the Heimlich maneuver different for children?
faq.a: Yes, adjust the force and hand placement based on the child's size, but the basic principle of abdominal thrusts remains the same for children over 1 year.

faq.q: What should you do if someone is choking?
faq.a: If conscious and unable to cough, speak, or breathe, apply the Heimlich maneuver immediately. If unconscious, begin CPR and call emergency services.

faq.q: Can you use the Heimlich maneuver on infants?
faq.a: No, infants under 1 year need a different technique: alternate 5 back blows with 5 chest thrusts. Never use abdominal thrusts on infants.

faq.q: Is the Heimlich maneuver safe during pregnancy?
faq.a: Modify the technique by using chest thrusts instead of abdominal thrusts to protect the abdomen and fetus.

faq.q: What do you do if the person becomes unconscious?
faq.a: Begin CPR immediately: 30 chest compressions followed by 2 rescue breaths. Call 911. Check the mouth for obstructions but do not blindly sweep the throat.

faq.q: When should you call 911 for choking?
faq.a: Call 911 immediately if the person cannot cough, speak, or breathe; if choking lasts more than a few minutes; or if the person loses consciousness.

faq.q: Are there alternatives to the Heimlich maneuver?
faq.a: Yes, back blows combined with chest thrusts work well, especially for infants and obese individuals. Some organizations recommend back blows as a first option.

faq.q: Should you get medical evaluation after a choking incident?
faq.a: Yes, always seek medical attention after a choking incident, even if the object is dislodged. Internal injuries or residual obstructions may need evaluation.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
